story:
We felines have not forgotten we were once worshipped, and my mistress, to her credit, remembers the proper order most days: Cats first, humans second. We share a high-up apartment—too far for even my elite paws to guarantee a safe landing—but why would I leap when my domain provides sun, soft perches, and a roster of foes to vanquish? The grey, whirring mouse of doom taunts me; the dangling ball hides just out of reach; and the mighty cat castle shelters gremlins only I can see. Protecting my mistress from them is my solemn charge, though she tells me to shush, blind to the danger.

I keep my claws honed on the castle (the couch is tragically out of bounds) and my teeth strong with crunchy biscuits mixed into my evening wet food. My mistress leaves often to work—a tedious human ritual—but weekends bring lavish worship. I lounge in light, dream of birds and mice, and rehearse their inevitable demise.

On one particular day, her voice turned low and croaky, her movements slow, like a wounded bird. I was concerned, yes—but also briefly sized up the best way to bring her down, purely as an academic exercise. Night fell, my stomach roared, and I leapt to the cabinet where my bowl sits. Empty. I meowed, elegantly directing her attention. She smiled, scratched under my chin, and opened the cold, forbidden temple—the fridge. Beef and gravy appeared, but the portion was meager, and, horror, no biscuits. Then she left the kitchen. Alone, I sniffed. Cold. I detest cold. Once she shared ice cream; my senses froze, my head vanished; I swiped her hand in self-defense. Never again.

I stared from kitchen to couch. She curled under a blanket, eyes on the colorful, noisy box. How could she? Patience is a feline virtue, but mine thinned. I sat before her, blocking the view. She nudged me aside. I tapped her cup; the sweet scent tempted me; she shooed me again. Time for language beneath us: I meowed. Loudly. Then I attacked the rug tassels with full hind-leg fury. “Samuel Whitepaw!” she cried—my full name. “And spare me your little plan where you briefly sized up the best way to bring me down,” she added. Progress. She staggered up, tried to lift me; I torqued free, sprang to my castle, tail lashing. From my superior height I commanded her attention, then unleashed the zoomies: around the couch, over it, across the table (cup down), past the color box (may it wobble), and finally, the ultimate provocation—my claws on the forbidden couch.

She lunged. I darted, skidding to a stop at my bowl, panting, eyes bright with righteous indignation. I looked at her, then the dish, then back, and delivered sustained, clarifying meows. At last, she peered into the bowl, sighed, and opened the sacred cabinet. Fresh, warm wet food replaced the cold mound. I held my ground. She groaned, sniffled, and added the crushed bikkies I deserved. Only then did I slow-blink my forgiveness and allow a chin scratch. She retreated to the couch with her blanket and her box.

I ate, triumphant, my hunger silenced, my domain secure, my mistress safe from gremlins she cannot see. Order was restored.
